learning_objectives.text: Define key concepts in infectious disease transmission including agent, host, and environment components of the epidemiological triad
Explain the mechanisms of host-pathogen interactions and how immune responses influence disease outcomes
Calculate and interpret basic reproduction numbers (R₀) and effective reproduction numbers (Rt) for infectious disease outbreaks
Construct and interpret epidemic curves to identify transmission patterns and outbreak characteristics
Assess the performance and effectiveness of surveillance systems for infectious disease detection and monitoring
Design outbreak investigation protocols incorporating contact tracing, laboratory confirmation, and control measures

key_topics.topic: Modes of transmission and chain of infection
Basic reproduction number (R₀) and effective reproduction number (Rt)
Surveillance systems and early warning indicators
Mathematical modeling in infectious disease control
Antimicrobial resistance as global health threat

case_studies.title: SARS-CoV-1 Outbreak Investigation and Global Response (2003)
case_studies.focus: Rapid outbreak investigation, international coordination, and containment strategies

case_studies.title: Nipah Virus Outbreak Investigation in Malaysia (1998-1999)
case_studies.focus: One Health approach, zoonotic disease investigation, and livestock-human transmission

case_studies.title: Global Influenza Surveillance Network Implementation
case_studies.who_region: Global (focus on WHO AFRO and SEARO regions)
case_studies.focus: Sentinel surveillance system design, laboratory networks, and pandemic preparedness

case_studies.title: Community-Based Vector Control Program - Dengue Prevention in Brazil
case_studies.focus: Community engagement, integrated vector management, and prevention effectiveness
